other_observers: Paul Neldner; Brandon Percival and Mark Peterson were here on 5/13/2011. Brandon will be submitting his own record and photos
description: At first glance we thought it was a Black-chinned Hummingbird...slender, small headed...definite black chin and white spot behind eye...then it flashed...ORANGE. Iridescent green back and forked tail. Tail was definitely longer than wings. Green mottled underparts with, extensive white fore-collar. Whitish median stripe down center of underparts.Behaviors: Bird was quite actively flashing and exhibiting territorial behavior...chasing off female Black-chinned hummingbirds but deferring to larger orioles. Fed extensively from small window feeder and larger feeder. Would light and look around for several moments at a time. Then would flash and display.Call: None heardPlumage: Male
photographer: Photographer: Polly Wren Neldner, Polly Neldner, Polly Wren Neldner, Polly Wren Neldner
